pi_items.source: Task description (line 6 in task.json) explicitly states 'Save the project to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/campfire_sim.blend'. Confirmed in setup_task.sh (line 13) OUTPUT_BLEND variable and export_result.sh (line 8).
Task description (item 3) requires 'Set the domain's Resolution Divisions to at least 32'. Verifier.py (line 100) checks 'if res >= 32', confirming this is the minimum threshold.

Task description (item 5) explicitly requires 'Set the scene frame range to Start=1, End=100'. Verifier.py (lines 111-114) checks both frame_start and frame_end with tolerance (start within ±5, end within ±20).

Task description (item 4) explicitly states 'use a 'Principled Volume' shader node connected to the Volume output'. No mention of VOLUME_SCATTER or VOLUME_ABSORPTION. Verifier.py (line 70 in export_result.sh) accepts ANY ONE of the three node types: 'if node.type in [PRINCIPLED_VOLUME, VOLUME_SCATTER, VOLUME_ABSORPTION]'. Blender documentation confirms Principled Volume is the modern unified volume shader that combines scattering and absorption (https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/shader_nodes/shader/volume_principled.html).

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic Blender Mantaflow configuration exercise with no external data. The agent must configure a fire and smoke simulation by: (1) creating a gas domain with minimum resolution 32, (2) adding a flow emitter set to fire type, (3) assigning a Principled Volume shader material to the domain, (4) setting frame range to 1-100, and (5) saving to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/campfire_sim.blend. The metadata claim that all three shader types (PRINCIPLED_VOLUME, VOLUME_SCATTER, VOLUME_ABSORPTION) are required is incorrect - the task description explicitly requires only Principled Volume, and the verifier accepts any one of the three.
